title: mod_rewrite Cheat Sheet
description: Essential mod_rewrite commands and patterns for Apache configuration.

commands.scenario: Use when you need to start using rewrite rules in your Apache configuration.

commands.description: Redirects oldpage.html to newpage.html with a 301 status code.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to permanently redirect a page to a new URL.

commands.description: Checks if the HTTP host is example.com, case-insensitive.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to apply rules based on the domain name.

commands.description: Temporarily redirects product URLs to newproduct URLs.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to temporarily redirect URLs during a site update.

commands.description: Checks if the requested filename is not a file.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to ensure the request is not for an existing file.

commands.description: Checks if the requested filename is not a directory.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to ensure the request is not for an existing directory.

commands.description: Stops processing if the request is for index.php.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to exclude index.php from further rewriting.
Use when you need to enforce HTTPS across your site.

commands.description: Matches query string with an 'id' parameter containing digits.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to apply rules based on query parameters.

commands.description: Rewrites blog URLs to a PHP script with a query parameter.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to map clean URLs to a script with parameters.

commands.description: Checks if the user agent starts with 'Mozilla', case-insensitive.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to apply rules based on the user agent.

commands.description: Redirects all requests to a maintenance page with a 503 status code.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to take the site offline for maintenance.
